title: Naked mole-rat queens use a chemical signal to suppress fertility in rivals
summary: There was one compound, an ester called isopropyl myristate, that scientists found in the samples taken from queens and nowhere else. “Breeding females had this particular molecule that non-breeding animals did not have,” Lewin said.
caption: output/2026-07-21/naked-mole-rat-queens-use-a-chemical-signal-to-suppress-fertility-in-r.txt
